attributes.description: Alerts when a tablespace utilisation exceeds 90%. Critical for SYSTEM/SYSAUX; write failures at full.
attributes.name: [Oracle OTel] Tablespace utilisation high

investigation_guide.blob:
## Oracle tablespace utilisation high

### What fired
Tablespace utilisation exceeded 90% during the window.

### Why it matters
Full tablespaces cause ORA-01653 / ORA-01654 write failures. SYSTEM/SYSAUX full is particularly dangerous.

### Triage
1. Identify large segments.
2. Check autoextend and maxsize.

### Remediation
- Extend datafiles or add new ones.
- Archive/purge historical data.

### Tuning
- 90% threshold; tighten for SYSTEM/SYSAUX.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-oracledbreceiver.otel-*
// Tablespace metrics are dimensioned by attributes.tablespace_name
| WHERE oracledb.tablespace_size.usage IS NOT NULL
        AND oracledb.tablespace_size.limit IS NOT NULL
| STATS usage = MAX(oracledb.tablespace_size.usage),
        limit_val = MAX(oracledb.tablespace_size.limit)
        BY oracledb.instance.name, host.name, attributes.tablespace_name
// Exclude unlimited tablespaces (limit -1)
| WHERE limit_val > 0
// Utilisation threshold: 90% — critical for SYSTEM/SYSAUX; write failures at full
// Adjust threshold per environment (e.g. 85 for stricter monitoring)
| EVAL utilisation_pct = ROUND(usage * 100.0 / limit_val, 1)
| WHERE utilisation_pct > 90
| SORT utilisation_pct DESC
